description_long: This is a vintage, red triplane with three stacked wings, reminiscent of early 20th-century fighter aircraft such as the Fokker Dr.I. The fuselage is cylindrical and tapers toward the tail, with a single open cockpit located just behind the upper wing. The wings and tail surfaces are painted red with black and white cross insignias, and the propeller is mounted at the nose. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The tail features a conventional design with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers, and the overall surface appears slightly weathered, suggesting a wooden and fabric construction.
description: A red, vintage triplane with three stacked wings, black and white cross insignias, and an open cockpit. The airplane features fixed landing gear and a conventional tail design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the three stacked wings, central propeller, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the open cockpit, propeller, and the cross insignias on the wings and tail.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tail assembly, horizontal and vertical stabilizers, and the triplane wing configuration.
description_view_3: Opposite side view showing the landing gear, cockpit, and the distinctive triple wing structure.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Triplane configuration with three straight, stacked wings in a mid-wing arrangement; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: single front-mounted piston engine with a two-blade propeller
attribute_tail_design: conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and two horizontal stabilizers
attribute_landing_gear_type: taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a tail skid
attribute_cockpit_placement: forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing
